title: subagent: hard-coded .openclaudia/permissions.json path couples PermissionManager to cwd
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/subagent.rs:789-793 (run_subagent)
## Violation
```rust
let permission_mgr = crate::permissions::PermissionManager::new(
    std::path::PathBuf::from(".openclaudia/permissions.json"),
    true,
    app_config.permissions.default_allow.clone(),
);
```

The subagent constructs its own PermissionManager from a hard-coded relative path. Three problems:

1. Temporal coupling to current working directory — a worktree-isolated subagent (which sets `cwd` indirectly via prompt injection at lines 709-715) will read/write the LEADER's permissions file, not its own.
2. A fresh PermissionManager per subagent invocation can't see in-memory "always allow" decisions made by the parent — each agent rebuilds an empty cache and re-prompts the user.
3. The choice of `true` for the second arg (presumably `enabled`) is not documented inline.

`Coordinator` is being built precisely to own a single shared PermissionManager (see coordinator/mod.rs:46 LeaderPermissionBridge), but `run_subagent` ignores it.

## Mandated refactor
Pass `&PermissionManager` into `run_subagent` as a parameter (either directly or as a field of `AppHandles`). Let the caller — eventually the Coordinator — supply the shared instance. Remove the hard-coded relative path entirely.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
